pattern: Rewriting the interior sum with prefix sums collapses each valid window into a hashable key, so one left-to-right pass counts matching left ends per right end.
hints: The condition ties together the two endpoint values and the prefix sums at their inner boundaries; express the interior sum with a prefix-sum array.
For a fixed right end r, a left end l works exactly when its endpoint value matches and a particular prefix-sum value (depending on r) is hit. That is a two-part key.

Sweep r from left to right; before counting at r, register the left end at index r-2 under the key formed by its value and the prefix sum just after it, then add the number of stored keys equal to the one r demands.

edge_inputs.description: negatives and zeros still satisfy the equation
minimal length, exactly one interior element
equal endpoints but interior sum differs

stress.description: moderate array over a narrow value range so equal endpoints recur and windows form
